This is a heavy testimony. Ali says that of this entire world, they held nothing but this land. It wasn't about the soil or the date palms; it was about the **Sanctity of the Prophetic Gift**. How was Fadak given to Fatima? When the verse was revealed:
And give the near of kin their due [Quran 17:26]
the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) called Fatima. Gabriel had told him:
Give Fatima Fadak.

This was not an inheritance that came after death; it was a **gift during his lifetime** (Nahlah). Yet the "Reality of the World" wanted to portray it as a disputed inheritance to apply their fabricated rule: **"Prophets do not leave inheritance."** By seizing Fadak, they were not just taking land; they were attempting to cut the financial lifeblood of the Imamate and humiliate the daughter of the Messenger.

They asked her for "witnesses" for a gift given by the Prophet! Does the "Leader of the Women of Paradise" need a witness for her truthfulness? The Quran says she is purified, yet they demanded witnesses to prove she wasn't lying. This is the "Chain of Lies" returning again. They rejected the testimony of Ali, and they rejected the testimony of her children, and they rejected her own word. This was the intellectual and political siege that preceded the physical assault.
Segment 3: The Logic of the Sermon and the Role of Aisha

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on the famous "Fadak Sermon" (al-Khutbah al-Fadakiyyah) delivered by Lady Fatima (SA) in the Prophet's Mosque. He describes the scene of her arrival, noting that her walk was identical to that of her father, which caused a state of emotional upheaval among the people. The Sheikh discusses how she used pure Quranic logic to dismantle the Caliph's fabrications, specifically highlighting her citations of the inheritance of previous Prophets. He also touches upon the "Document of Aisha," explaining how the testimony of the Prophet's wife was utilized to contradict Lady Fatima’s claims, further cementing the divide between the "Reality of the Progeny" and the "Political Reality" of the state.

The third point in this area is a glance at the **Sermon of Fadak**. Look at the atmosphere of this sermon. When Fatima (peace be upon her) decided to go to the mosque, she did not go as a mere "claimant" for property. She went as a **Manifestation of the Prophet**. The narrators say:
She put on her veil and her cloak, and she walked... and by Allah, her walk did not differ from the walk of the Messenger of Allah.
This wasn't just physical; it was a spiritual signal to the people that the one addressing them was the **Soul of Muhammad**.

When she entered the mosque and a curtain was drawn for her, she let out a single **Sigh**. That sigh was so heavy with grief and truth that the entire mosque erupted in wailing. People who had been silent or complicit could not hold back their tears. She silenced them and began her speech—a speech that is the **Alphabet of the Doctrine**. She didn't start with land. She started with **Tawhid**, **Prophecy**, and the **Wisdom of the Law**. Then she turned to them and said:
Are you now claiming that I have no inheritance? Is it in the Book of Allah that you inherit from your father, but I do not inherit from my father? Indeed, you have brought a monstrous thing!
She challenged them with the Quran. She cited the inheritance of **Solomon from David**, and the prayer of **Zechariah** for a son who would inherit from him. She asked them:
Did Allah reveal a verse specifically excluding my father? Or do you say that people of two different religions do not inherit from each other? Am I and my father not of the same religion?

This is the **Logic of the Infallible**. She exposed that their "Hadith" (Prophets do not leave inheritance) was a fabrication designed to override the Quran. It was a "theological weapon" used to strip her of her status.

And here we must mention the role of **Aisha**. The documents show that Aisha was one of the primary voices used to support the Caliph's claim. They used the "Mothers of the Believers" against the "Leader of the Women of Paradise." While Fatima was citing the Quran, they were citing a "private memory" that only the Caliph and Aisha seemed to share. This was the "Game of Sources" in its earliest, most lethal form. The "Reality of the World" needed Aisha's testimony to give a religious cover to a political theft. This created a rift that remains in the Ummah today. On one side, you have the **Quran and Fatima**; on the other, you have the **Fabricated Hadith and the Caliphate**.

Fadak was not an orchard. In the language of the Imams, Fadak is the **Wilayah** (Imamate). When the later Caliphs offered to return Fadak to the Imams, the Imams would define its boundaries as the entire Islamic world! Why? Because if you admit she was right about the land, you admit she was right about the **Leadership**. The "Slanders and Lies" were the only way they could keep their thrones.
Segment 4: The Symbolism of Fadak and Political Advisors

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the aftermath of the Fadak sermon, highlighting how the Caliph was momentarily moved by Lady Fatima's logic but was ultimately steered back by the political advisors around him. He details the theological claim made by Aisha regarding inheritance and how it was used to neutralize the Quranic evidence Lady Fatima had presented. The Sheikh explains that in the eyes of the Ahl al-Bayt, Fadak was never about material wealth; it was a symbol for the entire Islamic world and the right to lead it. He describes how this "theological theft" was a necessary precursor to the physical marginalization of Imam Ali (AS).

The sermon was not a plea for help; it was a trial. Fatima (peace be upon her) placed the entire Ummah in the witness stand. When she finished, the silence in the mosque was the silence of defeat. The documents tell us that Abu Bakr began to weep and said:
O daughter of the Messenger, you are the truth, and your father was the truth.
But the **Reality of the World** has a guard. Umar stood up and whispered to the Caliph:
If you give her Fadak today, your authority is over. You will have admitted that she is right and you are wrong, and that Ali is the true successor.

This is the political calculation. The truth was seen as a threat to the "Security of the State." Then came the use of **Aisha**. We find the reports explaining that Aisha was one of the primary reasons Lady Fatima was denied her inheritance. She provided the testimony that "supported" the Caliph's claim. They turned the house of the Prophet against itself. They used the status of one wife to strike the status of the only daughter.
Fatima’s logic was based on the **Universal Quran**, while their logic was based on a **Private Hadith** that no one else had heard. She said to them:
Does the Quran say Solomon inherited from David, or are you saying Solomon was not a Prophet?

They had no answer. But in the world of power, an answer is not needed when you have the sword. The Sheikh clarifies the true definition of the land. **Fadak isn’t merely an orchard; it is the Imamate.** In a famous narration, when Harun al-Rashid asked Imam al-Kadhim to define the boundaries of Fadak so he could return it, the Imam said:
The first boundary is Aden, the second is Samarkand, the third is Africa, and the fourth is the seas and the islands.
The Sultan was shocked and said:
Then you are asking for my entire kingdom!
The Imam replied:
I told you, if I define its true boundaries, you will never return it.

This is the "Reality of Religion." Fadak was the symbol of the **Total Authority** of the Ahl al-Bayt. By taking the orchard, they were declaring that the family of the Prophet had no right to anything in this world—neither land, nor leadership, nor even safety.
Segment 5: The Assassination Plot in the Mosque

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi reveals one of the most dangerous papers in the File: the premeditated plot to assassinate Imam Ali (AS) inside the mosque. He explains that after the Fadak sermon, the Saqifa party realized that as long as Ali remained alive, their authority was fragile. The Sheikh describes the secret meeting between the leaders and Khalid ibn al-Walid, who was assigned the task of killing the Imam during the morning prayer. He details the specific plan where Khalid was to stand next to Ali and strike him once the Caliph gave a specific signal at the end of the prayer. This document serves as proof that the "Reality of the World" was prepared to commit the ultimate sacrilege—murdering the Successor of the Prophet in the House of Allah—to protect their political project.

We now move to a paper that exposes the "Terrorist Side" of the Saqifa. This is not just about a land dispute; this is the **Plot to Assassinate the Commander of the Faithful**. The documents record that after the sermon of al-Zahra, the atmosphere in Medina became volatile. People were whispering, and the legitimacy of the new order was shaking. Abu Bakr and Umar met in private. Umar said:
As long as this man [Ali] is alive, you will not be secure on this pulpit. He must be removed.

They called for **Khalid ibn al-Walid**. Khalid was the "Sword of the State," the man who was willing to carry out the orders that others found too heavy. They made an agreement: Khalid would attend the morning prayer and stand right next to Ali. The plan was specific: Abu Bakr would lead the prayer. Khalid would have his sword hidden under his cloak. When Abu Bakr reached the final **Taslim** (the end of the prayer), he would give a signal, and at that moment, Khalid would draw his sword and strike Ali while he was still sitting.

Think about the "Reality of the World" here. They were planning a murder in the **Prophet's Mosque**, during the **Morning Prayer**, while they were standing before Allah! This shows you that they had no fear of Allah; they only feared losing the "Bride" (the World). As the prayer began, the documents say that Abu Bakr started to feel the weight of what he was doing. He was a man who often wavered when faced with the direct consequences of his actions. As he was reciting the prayer, he began to think about the aftermath. He realized that the Shi'a of Ali and the Hashimites would not remain silent; Medina would turn into a bloodbath.

The entire prayer passed in a state of terror. Khalid was waiting, his hand on the hilt of his sword, watching Abu Bakr’s mouth, waiting for the signal. Abu Bakr sat for the final **Tashahhud**, and the moment for the **Taslim** arrived. But Abu Bakr could not say it. He sat in silence for a long time. The people behind him were wondering why the Imam of the prayer was not finishing. Finally, out of fear, before saying the Salam, he spoke words that were not part of the prayer:
O Khalid! Do not do what I commanded you!
Then he said:
Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah.
Segment 6: The Reaction of Imam Ali and Theological Slander

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi describes the immediate aftermath of the failed assassination attempt. He details Imam Ali's (AS) reaction, specifically his physical confrontation with Khalid ibn al-Walid, and the subsequent "theological warfare" launched by the Saqifa party. The Sheikh presents a painful document from the teachings of Imam al-Sadiq (AS), which reveals that the enemies did not stop at physical assault but moved to strike the honor of Lady Fatima (SA). He explains how accusations were made from the pulpits against her character to justify their seizure of Fadak and their rejection of her testimony, framing this as a "Second Martyrdom" of the Prophet's daughter.

When Abu Bakr shouted those words—"O Khalid, do not do what I commanded you!"—the mosque was filled with confusion. People looked at one another, wondering what "command" was being cancelled in the middle of the prayer. The documents record that Imam Ali (peace be upon him) did not wait. He turned to Khalid, who still had his hand on his sword, and seized him. He threw him to the ground with such force that the people thought Khalid's bones would break. Ali looked at the two leaders and said:
Is this the 'Sunnah' of your Prophet? To kill the one he called his brother in his own mosque?

They had no answer but silence. But don't think they stopped there. When the "Sword" failed, they turned to the **"Tongue."** This is the most bitter paper in the File. Sheikh al-Ghizzi refers to a narration from our Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). The Imam says that they were not content with taking the land or breaking the rib. They wanted to destroy her **Status** in the eyes of the Ummah.
**Lady Fatima was accused of fornication (al-Fahisha) from their pulpits.**

Listen to the pain of the Infallible! Why did they do this? Because when she demanded Fadak, and they asked for witnesses, they began to insinuate that she was a "liar." And from calling the daughter of the Prophet a "liar," they moved to the most heinous of slanders. They used their "official media"—the Friday pulpits—to suggest that her claims were those of a woman who had lost her moral compass.

They did this to justify why they had rejected the **Verse of Purification**. They wanted to tell the people: "See? She is just an ordinary woman, prone to sin and error like anyone else." This was the "Intellectual Murder" of al-Zahra. It was a calculated move to ensure that the "Reality of the World" would never be threatened by her "Reality of Religion" ever again. This is why we say the File of Fatima is not just about a door. It is about a **System of Oppression** that used every weapon: the sword, the fire, the stolen land, and the poisoned tongue. They tried to bury her name under a mountain of slanders, but the "Light of Allah" cannot be extinguished by the breath of liars.
Segment 7: The Enforcer: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba

Sheikh al-Ghizzi transitions to a detailed examination of Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, whom he identifies as one of the primary "enforcers" and "criminals" of the Saqifa party. He describes Mughira as a man with a dark moral history, including a well-documented incident of adultery that was covered up by the authorities. The Sheikh explains that this lack of character made Mughira the perfect tool for the "Reality of the World" to use against the "Reality of Religion." He focuses on a specific historical document where Mughira, years later in the court of Mu'awiyah, attempted to verbally abuse Imam Hassan (AS), only to be met with a devastating public indictment. Imam Hassan reminded the court that Mughira was the one who physically struck Lady Fatima (SA) so severely that it led to the miscarriage of her child and her ultimate martyrdom.

Let us look at the "Tools of Power." Who were the men who stood around the pulpit? They weren't the men of piety; they were the men of the sword and the men of the "Dark Past." At the top of this list is **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba**. Mughira was a man whose history was so dark that even the early chroniclers couldn't hide it. He was a man accused of **Zina** (adultery) with four witnesses testifying against him. But because he was a "Vessel of Secrets" for the leadership, the legal punishment was avoided through a technicality provided by the Caliph.

Why is this important for the **File of Fatima**? Because a man who has no regard for the laws of Allah in his private life will have no regard for the "Leader of the Women of Paradise" in his public life. He was the "hitman" of the project. He was the one who was willing to strike when others hesitated. In a famous document from **Mu'awiyah's court**, Mughira tried to gain favor with the new Umayyad Sultan by insulting Imam Hassan (peace be upon him). He stood up and began to speak ill of Imam Ali and Lady Fatima, trying to reduce them to "rebels" against the state.
Imam Hassan (AS) rose with the dignity of his father. He didn't just defend himself; he exposed the **Crime Document** of the past. He looked at Mughira in front of the entire assembly and said:
And you, O Mughira! You are the one who struck Fatima, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, until she bled and miscarried what was in her womb!
The Imam said:
You did this to humiliate the Messenger of Allah and to oppose his command regarding her love.

This is a public accusation by an Infallible Imam. It confirms that the assault wasn't just a "push at the door"; it was a targeted, physical attack by men like Mughira who used their whips and their strength to break the core of the Prophetic family. Mughira’s silence after this indictment is his confession. The File of Fatima records his name in the ink of blood. He is the symbol of the **Lowness of the Attackers** versus the **Sublimity of the Attacked**. If you follow the "Sunnah" of Mughira, you are an enemy of the "Path of Fatima."
Segment 8: Institutionalized Abuse and Zaid ibn Ali

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the "culture of abuse" that was institutionalized against the Ahl al-Bayt, specifically focusing on the public cursing and belittling of Lady Fatima (SA) through the centuries. He links this historical animosity to the tragedy of Zaid ibn Ali, explaining how the Umayyad authorities used the same insults against Lady Fatima to justify the brutal execution of her descendants. He argues that the "File of Fatima" is a recurring tragedy where the descendants are punished for the "Sin of Loyalty" to the Prophet's daughter, proving that the original slanders were intended to have a permanent, lethal effect on the status of the Progeny.

We must understand that the assault on the house was not an isolated event. It was the birth of a **Culture of Abuse**. We note that the "Reality of the World" established a protocol where Lady Fatima’s name was mentioned with derision or intentional neglect from the pulpits of the Umayyads for decades. They did not just take the land; they took the **Honor of the Name**. Let us look at the incident of **Zaid ibn Ali ibn al-Hussain**. When Zaid rose against the tyranny of the Umayyads, he was not just fighting for a political cause; he was defending the honor of his grandmother, al-Zahra.

The documents of history record that when Zaid was brought before the tyrant **Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik**, the Sultan did not insult Zaid directly. Instead, he chose the most painful weapon. We see that he began to **insult Lady Fatima** in front of Zaid. He spoke words that no Muslim should ever utter about the "Leader of the Women of Paradise," questioning her status and her truthfulness. Zaid (peace be upon him) wept and said:
By Allah, if Muhammad were alive, would you dare speak of his daughter this way in front of his grandson?

But the "Reality of the World" has no shame. This incident proves that the **File of Fatima** was the tool used by the Caliphs to break the spirit of the Ahl al-Bayt’s followers. If they could desecrate the mother, they could destroy the sons.

We see the theological connection. If they could make the people believe that Fatima was "ordinary" or "mistaken" in the first century, then they could justify killing her grandsons in the second and third centuries. The "Slanders and Lies" of the Saqifa became the "Arrows and Swords" of the Umayyads and Abbasids. This is why the Ziyarah says: **"The one who killed you killed Islam."** Every drop of blood shed from the neck of Zaid ibn Ali, and every drop shed in Karbala, can be traced back to the blood that was spilled at the door of Fatima. The criminals are the same, and the motive is the same: the hatred for the "Prophetic Light" that refuses to submit to the "Darkness of the World."
Segment 9: The Veracity of the Progeny vs. The Umayyad Slander

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens the exploration of the "verbal assault" on Lady Fatima (SA) by examining the historical court of Mu'awiyah. He details the specific dialogue where Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba attempted to humiliate Imam Hassan (AS) through insults aimed at his parentage, which Imam Hassan countered by exposing Mughira's role in the physical attack on al-Zahra. The segment then moves to the Umayyad era, documenting how public cursing of Lady Fatima became a systematic tool to demoralize her descendants. The Sheikh recounts the tragic encounter between Zaid ibn Ali and the Caliph Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik, where the Caliph deliberately used heinous slanders against Lady Fatima to provoke and break the spirit of her grandson, illustrating that the "File of Fatima" remained a weapon of psychological warfare long after her death.

The documents of history show us that the "Reality of the World" never stopped at the door of the house. The assault continued through the tongues of the "Sultans' men." Let us return to the court of Mu'awiyah, where **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba** was sitting. Mughira thought he could use the pulpit to strike the **Status of the Progeny**. He began to speak about Imam Ali and Lady Fatima, using language that was meant to suggest they were "seekers of the world" or "rebels against the unity." But Imam Hassan (peace be upon him) was present.
The Infallible does not allow the Truth to be trampled. Imam Hassan rose and looked at Mughira—the man whose own life was a series of scandals—and said:
You speak of my mother? You, whom the Prophet cursed? You are the one who struck her side and broke her rib! Your hands are stained with the blood of her miscarriage!

This public indictment stripped Mughira of his mask. It shows that the "File of Fatima" was the primary defense of the Ahl al-Bayt against the liars. If you claim to be a Muslim, how can you sit with a man who admits to striking the daughter of your Prophet?

But the slanders did not end with Mughira. We move to a darker page: **The Swearing at Lady Fatima**. For nearly eighty years, the pulpits of the Umayyads were used to curse Imam Ali. But many people do not know that Lady Fatima was also a target of this institutionalized abuse. They wanted to erase the **Memory of her Purity**. If the people remembered that she was "The Leader of the Women of Paradise," they would realize that those sitting on the throne were usurpers. So they had to create a "New Fatima"—a woman who was "angry and stubborn" or "mistaken in her claims."

Look at the document of **Zaid ibn Ali**. When Zaid, the son of Imam al-Sajjad, was brought before **Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik**, the Caliph knew he couldn't defeat Zaid's logic. So he used the ultimate weapon of the Saqifa: the **Slander of al-Zahra**. The Caliph began to use foul language against Lady Fatima, saying:
What has the daughter of Muhammad brought us but rebellion?
and used even more graphic insults that I cannot repeat out of respect for her sanctity. Zaid wept and said:
By Allah, you have violated the Prophet in his grave!
The Caliph’s response was:
Her status died with her father; today, only the sword of the state remains.

This is the "Reality of the World." They believed that once the Prophet was gone, his daughter's honor was "fair game" for their politics. The File of Fatima records these insults as the **Second Martyrdom** of the Prophet's daughter.
Segment 10: The Abbasid Malice: Abu Ja'far al-Mansur

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on the Abbasid era, specifically the reign of Abu Ja'far al-Mansur (al-Dawaniqi). He highlights how the Abbasids, who initially rose to power using the slogan "The Pleasing one from the Family of Muhammad," became even more brutal towards the descendants of Lady Fatima (SA) than the Umayyads had been. The Sheikh provides documents showing how al-Mansur used the pulpit to publicly belittle Lady Fatima's status as a "mere woman" to deny the political and spiritual claims of her descendants. He argues that this systematic devaluation was a "re-opening" of the original wounds of the Saqifa, proving that both the Umayyad and Abbasid "Realities of the World" were unified in their mission to suppress the "Reality of the Progeny."

We move from the "Omelette of the Umayyads" to the "Fire of the Abbasids." If the Umayyads were the enemies who struck from the front, the Abbasids were the ones who betrayed from within. They used the name of the Ahl al-Bayt to steal the "Bride," and once they sat on the throne, they turned against the **Children of Fatima**. Look at the document regarding the Abbasid Caliph **Abu Ja'far al-Mansur**. He was a man of extreme malice toward the Fatimid lineage. When the Hasanid Sayyids rose in revolt, al-Mansur did not just fight them with soldiers; he fought them with the **"Weapon of Slander"**—the same weapon forged at Saqifa.
There is a recorded speech where al-Mansur stood on the pulpit and said:
What is the status of Fatima? She was but a woman. Does a woman inherit the Imamate? Does a woman grant legitimacy?

He was trying to decouple the **Prophetic Blood** from the **Prophetic Authority**. He wanted to convince the Ummah that being a descendant of Fatima carried no spiritual weight. By calling her "but a woman," he was echoing the words of the first Caliph who said she was "just a claimant" and the words of Umar who said "Even so!" regarding her presence in the house.

The documents show that al-Mansur went so far as to write letters to **Imam al-Sadiq** and the leaders of the revolts, mocking the fact that they took pride in a "female ancestor." This is the **Masculine Arrogance of the World** trying to extinguish the **Divine Light of the Womb of al-Zahra**. The "File of Fatima" records that under al-Mansur, the descendants of the Prophet were walled up alive in pillars of salt or buried in the foundations of buildings. Why such cruelty? Because their very existence was a reminder of the **Original Crime**. As long as a "Fatimid" existed, the Abbasid throne was a house built on sand.

They realized that to kill the sons, they had to "kill" the mother again—not with a door this time, but with **Theology**. They created a school of thought that marginalized the "Woman of Paradise" into a domestic figure with no relevance. This is the "Abbasid Poison" that still exists in many books today. The File of Fatima is here to pull her name out from those salt pillars and return her to the center of the Universe.
Segment 11: Persecution, Al-Mutawakkil, and Final Reflection

In the final segment of Episode 5, Sheikh al-Ghizzi connects historical slanders to the physical persecution of the Fatimid descendants. He details the uprising of Muhammad ibn al-Imam al-Sadiq and the treatment of the Prophet's family by the Abbasid Caliph al-Mutawakkil, who attempted to erase the memory of the Ahl al-Bayt through the destruction of Imam Hussain's grave. The Sheikh concludes with a deeply emotional personal reflection and a "monologue with al-Zahra," asserting that while everything in his life might be subject to error, his Love for Fatima remains the only absolute truth that will save him.

The "Reality of the World" reached its peak of madness in the era of the Abbasid Caliph **al-Mutawakkil**. If al-Mansur used theology to strike at Fatima, al-Mutawakkil used the **pickaxe and the plow**. The documents show that every revolution led by the descendants of Fatima—like that of **Muhammad the son of Imam al-Sadiq**—was fueled by the same fire: the refusal to let the slanders against their mother become the "Official Islam."

Al-Mutawakkil's crimes were not just against the living, but against the dead. We find the record of his order to **raze the grave of Imam Hussain**. He ordered the land to be plowed and water to be diverted over it so that no trace of the "Son of Fatima" would remain. Why did he do this? Because as long as the "Grave of the Oppressed" existed, the "Throne of the Oppressor" was in danger. He wanted to finalize the crime that started at the door of the house.

But look at the end of the tyrants and the end of the "Leader of Women." The Abbasids are in the trash-heap of history, while millions of hearts beat for the name of Fatima. I address you, O Mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussain: This is a page from the File that has been folded by the "Culture of Negligence," but we are here to unfold it. I say to you, O daughter of Muhammad: Everything I do is the "craft of the helpless." I have no power to give you your right. I am a man of many sins and errors.

I may lie about every single thing in my life I may lie in every claim I make except in one thing: **My Love for You, O Mother of the Pure Ones**. This love is my brain, my logic, and my very life. It is the breath that allows me to exist. Without your love, my heart is silent and my soul is void. This is my only hope: that when the "Time of the Departing Soul" comes, and I am placed in that dark grave, I will see you coming toward me with glad tidings. This is the hope of every "Fatimid." We live by your pain, and we hope to die by your pleasure. I am a friend to those who love you and an enemy to those who oppose you.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 6: The Personality of Umar ibn al-Khattab
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 6

In this sixth episode, Sheikh Al-Ghizzi shifts the focus of "The File" toward a psychological and behavioral analysis of the primary perpetrator, Umar ibn al-Khattab. He establishes a robust methodology for understanding character based on the concept of **Mu'ashara** (companionship/living with someone) and the accumulation of consistent behavioral reports that lead to self-evident truths. By meticulously examining Umar's interactions with the Prophet Muhammad, his fellow companions, and his treatment of women, the Sheikh constructs a profile of a man defined by harshness, intimidation, and a fundamental lack of spiritual sensitivity. This analysis provides the psychological context for Umar's actions during the attack on Lady Fatima’s house, arguing that his brutality was not an isolated incident but a manifestation of his established personality.
Segment 1: The Methodology of Knowing a Person

Sheikh Al-Ghizzi introduces the question of how we can truly know the character of an individual. He argues that the best method is Mu'ashara—living with a person through travel, hardship, and prosperity. He explains that consistent observation creates "self-evident" knowledge, similar to how we know the sun rises in the east. He links this to the logic of Tawatur (multiplicity of reports), asserting that when many different sources report the same behavioral patterns, the conclusion becomes certain.

Peace be upon Fatima al-Mustafa and al-Murtada after her and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place. **The File of Fatima - Episode 6.** How are we able to know a human being? When we want to know a person, there are several methods. Among these methods, and the best of them, is **Mu'ashara** (companionship). Living with a person closely allows us to know them better than any other way. Why is companionship the best method? You read in the books of jurisprudence about the ways to know the "justice" of a scholar or an Imam of prayer. One of these methods is companionship. When you live with a person, accompany them in travel and at home, in hardship and ease, in poverty and wealth, you become aware of their true nature.

When you conclude a person is truthful through companionship, how did you reach this result? What is the "secret" or the "technology" behind companionship? It is the **repetition of images**. Think of self-evident facts. Everyone knows the sun rises in the east. This doesn't require study; it is self-evident because of repetition. Since you first opened your eyes, you saw the sun in the east every morning. This is the secret. We conclude a person is honest, stingy, brave, or cowardly because the image of that quality repeats. We didn't use a microscope or perform a dissection; we simply observed the repeating pattern until it became a self-evident fact for us.

This is the same logic as **al-Tawatur al-Ma'nawi** (consecutive reporting of a meaning). Scholars of logic and mathematics agree that when a large group of people, who do not know each other and have no shared interest, all report the same thing, it produces certainty. The human soul reaches a degree of conviction because of the number and consistency of the reporters. This certainty can be in the literal words (Tawatur Lafzi) or in the underlying meaning (Tawatur Ma'nawi). For example, no Muslim doubts that Ali ibn Abi Talib was the bravest man of his time. Is there a single report with identical wording for every battle? No. But the multiplicity of different reports about his courage across various events produces a self-evident certainty. This is where we place the character of Umar. We do not look at one report; we look at the consistent image that repeats across his entire life.
Segment 2: Umar’s Behavior with the Prophet Muhammad

The Sheikh applies the methodology of companionship to Umar’s relationship with the Prophet. He highlights the consistent pattern of Umar challenging the Prophet's authority, most notably during the "Tragedy of Thursday." He argues that Umar's psychological profile was one of "perpetual opposition," where he viewed himself as a corrective force to the Prophet's decisions. The Sheikh describes this not as "independent reasoning," but as a fundamental lack of Adab (etiquette) and submission to the Divine Messenger.

Let us look at the "companion" of the Prophet. The history books—even those of his supporters—record a consistent image of Umar ibn al-Khattab. We see a man who frequently raised his voice over the Prophet, leading to the revelation of verses in Surah al-Hujurat. We see a man who doubted the Prophet's judgment at Hudaybiyyah, famously saying:
Are you not truly the Messenger of Allah?

This behavior reached its peak during the Prophet's final illness, in the event known as the **"Tragedy of Thursday."** When the Prophet asked for a pen and paper to write a decree that would save the Ummah from straying, Umar was the one who blocked him. He said:
The Prophet is delirious, [or] Pain has overcome him.

Look at the "Reality of Religion" vs the "Reality of the World" here. Umar was making a political calculation: he knew the Prophet wanted to write a document confirming Ali’s leadership. To prevent this, he had to strip the Prophet of his "Infallible Logic" in the eyes of the people present. This is the repeating image: a man who is ready to insult the Prophet on his deathbed to secure a political goal. It shows a psychological profile that does not recognize "Sanctity." If he was capable of speaking this way to the Messenger of Allah himself, why would anyone find it "doubtful" that he would strike the Messenger's daughter? The character is consistent. The person who says "the Prophet is delirious" is the same person who says "Even so!" when told Fatima is inside the house.
Segment 3: The View of the Companions and the "Broker"

Sheikh Al-Ghizzi examines how other companions perceived Umar. He discusses reports where companions expressed fear of his temper and harshness. He then explores an often-overlooked aspect of Umar's pre-Islamic life: his role as a "broker" or "mediator" in commercial dealings. The Sheikh argues that this background in "haggling" and "coarseness" shaped his approach to leadership, where he treated the Caliphate as a business deal rather than a spiritual trust.
How did the other companions view him? History tells us they were terrified of his whip. When Abu Bakr appointed Umar as his successor, some companions came to him and asked:
What will you say to your Lord, having appointed over us a man so harsh and severe?

Even those who supported the Saqifa were afraid of the "Reality" of Umar's personality. We should look at his origins. In the days of Jahiliyyah, Umar was known as a **Simsar**—a broker or a middleman. A broker’s life is built on negotiation, loud voices, and hard bargaining. It is a profession of the "Market," not the "Temple." He brought this "Market Mentality" into Islam. For him, the pledge of allegiance was a deal to be closed, by any means necessary. He didn't have the spiritual refinement of those raised in the light of the Prophet's house. This is why he could not understand the "Reality of Religion" that Fatima represented.
Segment 4: The Tree of the Messenger and the Drunkards

The Sheikh narrates two revealing incidents. First, he describes how Umar ordered the cutting down of the "Tree of the Pledge" because people were visiting it for blessings, showing his "Enmity toward Relics." Second, he discusses incidents where Umar was reportedly corrected or even refuted by individuals caught drinking wine. The Sheikh uses these to show that Umar’s "harshness" was often a cover for his own lack of knowledge and spiritual depth.

There is the incident of the **Tree of the Ridwan Pledge**. Because people were gathering there to pray and seek blessings, Umar ordered it to be cut down. He wanted to erase anything that connected the hearts to the "Signs of Allah" if he couldn't control it. This is the "Dry Mentality." Then we have the reports of the **Drunkards**. There were cases where Umar would try to punish people for drinking, and they would refute him using the Quran! One man told him:
You have spied on me, and Allah forbade spying. You entered without permission, and Allah commanded to enter through doors.

Umar was left silent. This shows that his "Reality of the World" was not built on Quranic wisdom, but on physical intimidation. When someone stood up to him with the Book, he had no answer. But Fatima stood up to him with the Book, and his answer was the fire.
Segment 5: Imam Ali’s Analysis of the Shura

Sheikh Al-Ghizzi analyzes the "Shura" (committee) formed by Umar to choose his successor. He cites Imam Ali’s analysis of this committee as a "rigged game" designed to ensure the Caliphate would never return to the Ahl al-Bayt. The Sheikh argues that the Shura was the final "Intellectual Crime" of Umar, ensuring that the path of the "Reality of the World" would continue long after his death.

Look at how Umar handled his death. He formed a committee of six. Imam Ali said in the **Shiqshiqiyya** sermon that Umar knew Ali was the most qualified, yet he placed him in a committee with people who were his rivals. Umar gave the tie-breaking vote to Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, knowing that he was the brother-in-law of Uthman. The game was set. This shows a man who was calculating until his last breath. He wanted to "lock the door" of leadership against Ali, just as he had literally "broken the door" of Fatima.
Segment 6: Umar and Women: A History of Violence

In this harrowing segment, the Sheikh examines Umar's behavior toward women throughout his life. He details the incident of Umar beating his sister and her husband when he first discovered they had accepted Islam. He also discusses reports of Umar’s failed proposals to the daughters of companions, where he was rejected because of his "harshness with women." The Sheikh argues that this "Misogynistic Brutality" was a core part of his character.

Now we reach the most relevant part of his character for the File of Fatima: his behavior with women. We look at his own conversion story. When he went to his sister’s house and found them reading the Quran, what did he do? He didn't debate. He struck his sister until her blood flowed. This was his "First Reaction"—violence against his own flesh and blood.

There is the report of him confessing to **burying his daughter alive** during Jahiliyyah. He said he wept while she was wiping the dust off his beard. Then look at his behavior with **Umm Abdullah**. When she was preparing to migrate to Abyssinia, he spoke to her with such harshness that she feared him. He tried to marry the daughter of Abu Bakr, and she refused. When asked why, it was said:
He is a man of harsh life and hard treatment toward women.

He tried to marry the daughter of Ali, and the reports show the same pattern of intimidation and refusal. The image is consistent: a man who uses his physical strength to dominate and terrify the women around him. This is the psychological bridge to the event at the door.
Segment 7: Domestic Violence and the Silencing of Mourning

The Sheikh details incidents where Umar reportedly used physical force against his own wife and other women who were mourning. He describes an event where Umar forbade Aisha and other women from weeping for the death of Abu Bakr, using his whip to strike them. The Sheikh argues that this "Enmity toward Grief" and "Violence toward Mourners" was a way to suppress the emotional truth of the community.
There are reports in the Sahihs of him **beating his wife** at night and then telling people:
Do not ask a man why he beats his wife.
Then we see his behavior during times of mourning. When Abu Bakr died, the women were weeping. Umar came with his whip and began to strike them. He even struck Aisha! He told them:
The dead are punished by the weeping of the living.

He did the same with the mourners of Khalid ibn al-Walid. This is a man who wanted to control the very tears of the people. He once heard a woman wailing in the street at night and went out to stop her with his whip. After seeing all these repeated images, what is the conclusion? We have a man who is physically violent, spiritually dry, and inherently harsh toward women.
Segment 8: Conclusion and the Poetry of the Progeny

Sheikh Al-Ghizzi concludes that the "File of Fatima" is entirely consistent with the historical character of Umar. He recites moving lines from the poem Anwar al-Qudsiya and the verses of Sheikh Salih al-Kawaz, linking the broken rib of Fatima to the tragedy of Karbala. He ends with an emotional prayer for the appearance of the Mahdi, the avenger of Fatima.

We conclude today. The character of the man is clear. **He was the "Man of the Market," the "Man of the Whip."** When we say he broke the rib of al-Zahra, we are not inventing a story. We are describing an action that fits perfectly with every other page of his life. I end with the words of **Sheikh Salih al-Kawaz**:
The most painful thing is that the heart of Muhammad is grieved... The oath was taken to build the throne on the blood of the Progeny.
If it were not for the falling of Fatima's fetus [Mohsen], no forehead would have been struck with blood in Karbala.
The whip that wrapped around Fatima’s arm became the lash that struck the daughters of Hussain.

O daughter of Muhammad, this is our effort. It is the effort of the helpless. We wait for the appearance of your son, the Hujja, to bring the final justice. Peace be upon you all, O Fatimids. Until we meet in the final episode.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 7: The Sacrifice of Fatima
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 7

In this final episode, titled **"The Sacrifice of Fatima,"** Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes his investigation into the "File of Fatima" by exploring the spiritual and historical dimensions of the Prophet's final Will (al-Wasiyya). He analyzes why Imam Ali (AS) was commanded to remain patient despite the physical assault on his home, framing it within a "Philosophy of History" where extraordinary moments dictate the future of nations. The episode moves from theological proofs into a deeply emotional **Munajat** (intimate prayer), asserting that the File remains open and will only be finally resolved by the appearance of the Mahdi (AS).
Segment 1: The Salawat of Imam al-Askari

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the series finale with a poetic salutation to Lady Fatima. He begins the formal discussion by citing a specific Salawat (blessing) authored by the 11th Imam, Hassan al-Askari (AS), found in the "Blessings upon the Pure Proofs" section of Mafatih al-Jinan. This text serves as a theological document that explicitly identifies Lady Fatima as "The Truthful One" and calls upon Allah to seek justice against those who "belittled her right" and "oppressed her."
The Prophet said:
May your father be your ransom, O Zahra.

O Zahra, she who is the lamp of creation. Peace be upon Fatima, and al-Mustafa her father, and al-Murtada her husband, and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place. **The File of Fatima - Episode 7.** The final episode of the segments of this program.

The first thing I open my words with is the speech of our Imam, the Pure Hassan al-Askari (peace be upon him). There are important **Salawat** mentioned by al-Muhaddith al-Qummi in **Mafatih al-Jinan** under the title "Prayers upon the Pure Proofs." These are narrated from Imam al-Askari, with a specific prayer for each Infallible. I will open with what came in the section for the Mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussain.
Our Imam teaches us to say:

O Allah, bless the Truthful One, Fatima, the Pure, the beloved of Your Beloved and Your Prophet, and the mother of Your loved ones and Your chosen ones... whom You selected and preferred over the women of the worlds.
O Allah, be the Seeker of justice for her from those who oppressed her and belittled her right. O Allah, be the Avenger for the blood of her children.

O Allah, just as You made her the Mother of the Imams of Guidance, the partner of the Owner of the Banner [Ali], and the noble one in the Highest Assembly... bless her and her mother with a blessing that honors the face of her father Muhammad... and brings delight to the eyes of her progeny.
Peace be upon you, O Mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussain. Peace be upon you, my master and Imam, Abu Muhammad al-Zaki al-Askari.
Segment 2: The Covenant of Patience and Extraordinary Time

The Sheikh outlines the three main directions of the final episode: 1) The Prophet’s Will to Ali regarding patience, 2) Visual evidence and "snapshots" for the file, and 3) The conclusion of the series. He introduces the "Covenant of Patience," which is often questioned by believers: "Why did Ali not fight back?" To answer this, he utilizes the "Philosophy of History," distinguishing between "Routine Time" and "Extraordinary Time." He argues that the moment of the attack was an extraordinary juncture where Ali's reaction had to be governed by a Divine program to preserve the core of the religion, rather than a standard emotional or physical response.

My talk in this episode falls into several directions. The first direction will be about the **Will** (al-Wasiyya) that is frequently mentioned in the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt and about which the Shi'a often ask: The Prophet's Will to Ali (AS) regarding patience and swallowing one's anger. As Sayyid Rida al-Hindi said:
I wish you were not commanded to be patient.

This is a wish many have. The second direction consists of "snapshots" or images to be added to the file. Just as every file has documents and photos, I will add these to the memory of the believers. After all that, I will go to a conclusion, though this file never truly closes. It will only be closed by the Imam of our Time (AS).

The first direction: The Will. "Ali was bound and tied by the ropes of the Will"—this meaning is constantly repeated. What is this Will? In its essence, it is **Patience**. This is the general title. When we look at matters, they are of two types: ordinary matters of daily routine, and extraordinary matters with a specific "particularity."

Philosophers of history, when they study the path of nations, find that **Time** is the most important factor. I do not mean time as a physical state resulting from the movement of the earth, but rather the human interaction with time. Philosophers of history divide time into "Routine Time" and "Extraordinary Time." If certain events occur during **"Extraordinary Time,"** they have a massive impact on the path of the nation. These are the junctures that lead to great changes, for better or worse.

When we want to analyze a matter, we must diagnose it: is it an ordinary matter or an extraordinary one? The "Will" given to Ali was not for a routine event; it was a program for the most extraordinary crisis in the history of Islam, where the "Reality of the World" attempted to swallow the "Reality of Religion" entirely. He told Ali:
O Ali, you will face after me what will make the mountains crumble... but you must be patient until you find no way but to fight.

If Ali had drawn his sword at the door, the history books would have recorded that Ali turned into a "rebel" against the "Legitimate State." They would have erased the name of Muhammad using Ali’s own sword.
Segment 3: The Prophetic Program and the Sacrifice of the Imamate

Sheikh al-Ghizzi elaborates on the nature of the Prophetic "Program" that was introduced to transition the Ummah into a state of spiritual and political perfection. He argues that the events at Saqifa and the subsequent attack on the home were not merely political disputes but a systemic rejection of a Divine blueprint. He discusses how this interruption created a historical "gap" that altered the course of Islamic history, explaining that the "Will" was the only way to safeguard the remaining essence of Islam from total annihilation at the hands of those who sought only temporal power.

We must look at the program the Prophet brought. It was a program for the entirety of human existence. When this program was struck at Saqifa, it was the breaking of a Divine path. The "Will" of patience given to Ali was the most difficult part of this program. This was not a lack of courage—it was the peak of courage. It was the courage to fulfill a Divine command that he knew would lead to his own physical and social marginalization.
If Ali had fought back, the enemies would have said:
Look, Muhammad is dead and now his family is fighting for his throne.

They would have turned the religion into a mere "Family Inheritance." By staying patient, Ali forced the crime to be recorded as a **One-Sided Assault**. This is what we call the "Sacrifice of the Imamate." The Imamate was sacrificed in its political form so that it could survive in its spiritual and legislative form. Lady Fatima was the primary participant in this sacrifice. She was the one who went to the door to protect Ali, and she was the one who endured the blow so that Ali would not have to draw his sword and break the "Will."
The documents of history show that the Ummah entered a "Dark Age" from that moment. The Saqifa wasn't a "consultation"; it was a coup against the Heavens. The Prophet had said:
I am leaving among you two weighty things: The Book of Allah and my Progeny.

At Saqifa, they attempted to separate the "Silent Quran" from the "Speaking Quran." Ali stayed patient so that we, centuries later, would have the chance to recognize who the true successors were. Every hardship faced by the Imams, from the poisoning of Imam Hassan to the slaughter of Imam Hussain, is a branch of the "Will." The File of Fatima is the "Mother Document" of all these sacrifices.
Segment 4: Legislation, Creation, and the Secret of the Rib

In this segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores the connection between Tashri’ (Legislation) and Takwin(Creation). He argues that the Prophetic "Program" was a divine blueprint intended to align human life with the cosmic laws of the universe. He explains that the "Will of Patience" was a mechanism to maintain a link to the Divine in a world that had severed its connection to the "Source of Light." He emphasizes that the suffering of the Prophet's daughter was a cosmic sacrifice, intended to preserve the spiritual "Seed" of the Imamate within a hostile material reality.

We now reach a point that requires deep reflection: the connection of **Legislation with Creation** (Tashri' and Takwin). The program brought by the Messenger of Allah was an extension of the very laws that govern the stars, the angels, and the souls. When we speak of the "Progeny," we are speaking of the **Pillars of Creation**. Therefore, when the "Reality of the World" rose to strike the house of Fatima, it was an attempt to disconnect human history from its divine purpose.

The "Will" of patience was a **Cosmic Necessity**. If the link between the Creator and the Created had been completely severed, the light of guidance would have been extinguished forever. Ali (AS) accepted the "Ropes of the Will" to keep the path of the "Reality of Religion" open. Why was it Fatima? Why was she the one to stand behind the door? Because she is the **Secret of the Program**. She is the bridge between Prophecy and Imamate.

By breaking her rib, they were attempting to break the link between the Ummah and the Heavens. Ali’s silence allowed the "File of Fatima" to be written in the blood of truth. He allowed the world to see the contrast: the **Brutality of the Usurper** versus the **Patience of the Divine Successor**. Lady Fatima gave her life, her health, and her child to ensure that the "Program" would not be entirely lost. She became the "Guardian of the Secret" (al-Sirr al-Mustawda’).
Segment 5: The Letter to Abu Bakr and the Sacrifice of Lut

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents Imam Ali's (AS) Letter to Abu Bakr, in which the Imam explicitly challenges the legitimacy of the coup. The Sheikh then draws a Quranic parallel to the Sacrifice of Prophet Lut (AS), explaining that just as Lut was willing to sacrifice his family to protect his "Divine Guests" from a mob, Imam Ali and Lady Fatima sacrificed their safety to protect the "Divine Guests" of Islam—the Quran and the True Message. This frames the "Will of Patience" as a deliberate choice to prioritize Divine Truth over physical survival.
We now turn to a document of immense historical weight: the **Letter of the Commander of the Faithful to Abu Bakr**. In this letter, Ali (AS) says:
By Allah, I did not think that the Arabs would turn the matter away from the Household of the Prophet... or that they would snatch it from me after him.
He describes the atmosphere as a **Theft of the Prophetic legacy**.
Look at the Quranic connection to the **Sacrifice of Prophet Lut**. Look at the verse where the people of Sodom surrounded Lut's house, demanding his guests. Lut said:
O my people, these are my daughters, they are purer for you... do not disgrace me in my guests.
Why did a Prophet offer his own daughters to a mob? It was a **Sacrifice for the Guests of Allah**. Lut was willing to let his family suffer to protect the "Sanctity of the Mission."

Imam Ali and Lady Fatima were the "Lut" of this Ummah. Their "Guests" were the **Quran** and the **Final Prophecy**. When the mob surrounded the house with fire and wood, Ali and Fatima allowed their house to be violated so that the "Divine Guests"—the Truth—would not be entirely erased. Ali’s patience was the "Rope of Lut." He watched Fatima stand at the door to be the shield for the Prophet’s Guests. Lady Fatima gave her rib and her child so that the world would know: the "Reality of the World" can break the body, but it cannot touch the "Guests of Allah."
Segment 6: Snapshots, Munajat, and the Unending File

In the concluding segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi brings the series to a spiritual climax. He moves from historical analysis to a heartfelt Munajat with Lady Fatima (SA), reflecting on her physical suffering and connecting it to the tragedy of Karbala. He asserts that his work is merely the "effort of the helpless" and declares that the "File of Fatima" remains a living grievance that will only be settled by the Mahdi (AS).

The sacrifice of Lut was a shadow of the sacrifice of Ali. We reach the end of the research, but we do not reach the end of the grief. O Mother of the Pure Ones! O daughter of the Messenger of Allah! We have turned these pages, but our hearts are still heavy with the "Secret of the Rib." The "File of Fatima" is not a history book. It is a living wound.
The Sheikh recites the verses of **Sheikh Salih al-Kawaz**:

If it were not for the falling of Fatima's fetus, no child would have been slaughtered in Karbala... The fire that burned her door was the same fire that consumed the tents of the daughters of Hussain.
O Zahra! This is the **effort of the helpless**. I have no power to restore your right. My only honor is to be counted among your servants.
The document says:
Umar prevented her from weeping... he cut down the Arak tree where she sought shade.

Sufficient for me is to be one of your slaves, O Fatima. If I have achieved anything, it is by your grace. The File of Fatima is not closed. It is waiting for the one who will open it with the hand of justice. We wait for your son, the Mahdi.

title: The File of Ali - Episode 1: The Will
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In this inaugural episode of the new series, Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores the foundational concept of the **Will (Wasiyya)**. Moving away from technical grading of transmission chains, he argues that leaving a will is a self-evident human instinct (**Fitra**), making it irrational to claim the Prophet Muhammad (SAWA) abandoned his Ummah without clear leadership. The episode delves into primary historical documents, primarily from Sunni sources like **Tarikh al-Tabari**, to expose the hypocrisy of the early Caliphs who actively secured their own successions while denying the Prophet's right to do the same. The Sheikh also unveils the "Documentary Theft" used by later historians to censor Imam Ali's clear, early appointment as the **Wasi** (Executor) and **Khalifa** (Successor).
Segment 1: The Instinct of the Will (Fitra)

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens with a spiritual salutation to the "Face of Allah" and the followers of "Abu Turab" (Ali). He declares that this new series will not be a technical exercise in grading chains of narration but an exploration of self-evident truths found in history and the human soul. He argues that the concept of a Will (Wasiyya) is a natural instinct (Fitra); just as a parent or a child instinctively provides instructions before leaving, it is irrational to believe the Prophet would leave the core of Islam to chance. He then introduces historical evidence from Tarikh al-Tabari showing that even those who denied the Prophet's Will felt the need to write their own wills to secure power.

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Peace be upon the Radiant **Face of Allah** and His High Side. Peace be upon the ripe fruit and the brilliant star. Peace be upon **Abu al-Hassan Ali**, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. Peace be upon you, O **Shi'a of Abu Turab**, in every corner of this existence—living and dead, young and old, men and women.

**The File of Ali - Episode 1.** The beginning is from the **Fitra** (innate nature) and the conscience (al-Wujdan). As was mentioned in "The File of Fatima," I will not play the game of "chains and sources"—this frail, spider-web-like game. The Truth is clear and explicit, and the logic of reality points to itself. I will browse pages from our history that still contain many clues despite all the distortion and can be found here and there.

The **Will** is a matter judged by the **Fitra** and the conscience, regardless of what is said or what weak excuses or "interpretations" are brought. Traditionists, theologians, and historians have spoken at length—sometimes truthfully, sometimes distorting or hiding facts. But the Fitra, the conscience, and eventually the **Reason** remain the clear evidence and the shining proof that accepts no debate. Yes, stubbornness can come and cover the evidence of the conscience, but it is a covering that is not real; inside the person, the matter is clear and evident.

No sane person who has the slightest level of concern for any affair in their life—if they know they will not continue to oversee it due to illness, travel, or death—would fail to leave a will. The conscience, the Fitra, and the Reason all command the necessity of a will here. A person is driven to make a will in such circumstances by an innate motive without even thinking.

This innate drive is present even in children. If a child leaves their toys scattered and has to go out, they will tell someone in the house to look after their toys. Even if they travel, they will call back to ask about their needs and things they care about. This is the logic of the human soul. Is it then conceivable that the Master of all human souls—indeed, the Master of all beings, **Muhammad** (peace be upon him and his family)—would depart from this natural system? Would he leave the most important matter of his life—Islam, faith, the Ummah, guidance, and misguidance—to be played with by whims and ignorant souls? It is impossible.

When we browse history, what do we find? We find it in the very history of those who denied the Will and said that Muhammad did not appoint anyone. For example, let us go to **Tarikh al-Tabari** (Volume 2, page 591), regarding the final days of the first Caliph, Abu Bakr, during his terminal illness. He called Uthman into a private meeting and said:
Write: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. This is what Abu Bakr ibn Abi Quhafa has covenanted to the Muslims...
He felt the natural, innate need to secure the succession, yet they claim the Messenger of Allah did not do the same for his Ummah.
Segment 2: The Double Standard of Succession

Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to analyze the historical records of the early successions to demonstrate the hypocrisy of denying the Prophet’s Will. He focuses on the appointment of Umar by Abu Bakr, noting that Abu Bakr did not leave the matter to "consultation" (Shura) but made a definitive, written appointment while he was drifting in and out of consciousness. The Sheikh then highlights the irony: while the Prophet's request for a pen and paper on his deathbed was blocked by Umar with the claim that "pain has overcome him," the same group accepted Abu Bakr’s written decree under similar medical circumstances. He describes the atmosphere of Umar’s succession as one of intimidation.

We find that the same people who claimed the Prophet left the Ummah without a successor were the most eager to appoint their own successors. Look at the text in **Tarikh al-Tabari**. It says that Abu Bakr fainted while dictating the document. Uthman, seeing him unconscious, feared he would die without finishing, so Uthman wrote Umar’s name on his own initiative.
When Abu Bakr woke up, he asked: "What did you write?" Uthman told him he had written Umar’s name. Abu Bakr replied:
May Allah reward you with good, for if you had written your own name, you would have been worthy of it.

Where was the **Shura** (consultation) here? Where was the "consensus of the companions"? It was a private appointment. But the point I want to emphasize for your conscience is the double standard. When the Messenger of Allah, who is "protected from error," asked for a pen to write a document that would prevent misguidance, Umar stopped him and said:
The man is delirious.

Yet, when Abu Bakr was literally fainting and losing consciousness—actually delirious from the agony of death—they accepted his document as the "Covenant of the Caliphate." This is the **Reality of the World**. They didn't reject the concept of a Will; they only rejected the **Prophet's Will** because it named Ali.

Then we move to the Will of Umar. When he was dying, he created a committee of six men. But look at the language used—it was a will full of blood and threats. He gave instructions that if the committee did not agree within three days, the minority should be beheaded. If they were split three-against-three, the side that didn't have **Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf** should be killed.

Is this the "Religion of Mercy"? A succession plan based on the threat of the sword against the elite companions of the Prophet? This demonstrates that even the leaders of Saqifa knew that without a "Will," the state would collapse. They followed their Fitra to protect their worldly power, but they fought against the Fitra when it came to the **Divine Right** of the Household of Muhammad.

So, the question is not "Did the Prophet leave a Will?" The question is **"Why did they suppress it?"** History proves that the principle of succession was practiced by everyone—except, according to their claim, the one who was the most responsible for the Ummah's future. This logic is shattered by the historical documents found in their own books.
Segment 3: The Universal Sunnah and The Day of Warning

Sheikh al-Ghizzi argues that the appointment of a successor is a Universal Sunnah (practice) of all Prophets since Adam. He cites the principle that "every Prophet has a Wasi (executor/successor)" and asserts that Muhammad (PBUH) could not be the exception. The Sheikh then turns to the "First Document" of Ali's appointment: the Day of the Warning (Yawm al-Inthar), where the Prophet gathered his relatives at the very beginning of his mission and declared Ali as his brother, executor, and successor. He highlights how this early appointment was deliberately distorted in works like Tarikh al-Tabari.

Let us look at the "Universal Logic." The Sheikh establishes a vital principle: **Every Prophet had a successor.** This is a historical and theological fact found in both the Quran and the narrations of all Abrahamic faiths. Adam had Seth, Noah had Shem, Abraham had Ishmael and Isaac, Moses had Aaron and Joshua, and Jesus had Simon Peter.

Is it logical that the "Seal of the Prophets," who brought the final and most perfect religion, would be the only one to leave his people in chaos? The **Reality of Religion** dictates that the succession of Ali was not a late political decision made at Ghadir Khumm; it was a **Primal Appointment** that began with the very first call to Islam.

We find the "First Paper" of this file in the incident of **al-Dha'wah al-Zul-Qurba** (The Call to the Near Kin). When the verse was revealed: "And warn your nearest kinsmen" [Quran 26:214], the Prophet gathered the children of Abd al-Muttalib. He offered them a deal:
Who among you will support me in this matter, to be my brother, my executor (Wasi), and my successor (Khalifa) among you?

The documents record that no one stood up except the youngest among them—Ali. The Prophet repeated the offer three times, and each time, only Ali stood up. At that moment, the Prophet placed his hand on Ali’s neck and said to the tribal leaders:
This is my brother, my executor, and my successor among you. Listen to him and obey him!

This was at the dawn of Islam, in Mecca, years before the migration. The appointment of Ali was the "Second Pillar" of the message after Tawhid. But look at what happened to this document. The Sheikh exposes the distortion in **Tarikh al-Tabari**. In some editions of his history, and especially in his Tafsir, Tabari changed the words of the Prophet.

Instead of recording the Prophet’s clear words: "my executor and successor" (Wasiyi wa Khalifati), they changed it in the printed books to: "He is my so-and-so and so-and-so" (Kaza wa Kaza). They tried to **erase** the appointment from the text itself! This is the **"Documentary Theft"** that has been practiced for centuries to hide the "Reality of Ali." They knew that if the people read the truth about the beginning of the mission, they would realize that the Caliphate was never a matter for "Shura" or election—it was a Divine Decree made from day one.
Segment 4: The Constant Education and the Position of Aaron

Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to document the "continuous appointment" of Imam Ali (AS) throughout the life of the Prophet. He argues that the titles "Executor" (al-Wasi) and "Successor" (al-Khalifa) were names used for Ali in the presence of the Prophet. He counters attempts to narrow the scope of these words by presenting the logic that a Prophet’s words are part of a universal legislative program. He also discusses the "Position of Aaron" (Hadith al-Manzila) as a permanent rank.

The appointment was not a single event; it was a **Constant Education**. The Prophet (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) did not just say "Ali is the Successor" once. He wove this reality into the very fabric of the community's life.

Look at the title **"al-Wasi"** (The Executor). In the Arabic language of that time, every tribal leader had an executor—the person who would settle his debts, fulfill his promises, and stand in his place. When the Prophet consistently called Ali "my Wasi," he was using a legal and social term that everyone understood as the **Designated Heir of Authority**.
We find reports even in the Sahihs where people asked:
Who is the executor of Muhammad?

The very fact that the question was asked proves that the concept of an executor was expected and known. But the "Reality of the World" tried to create a vacuum. They tried to claim the Prophet died "without making a will," which contradicts both his character and the Quranic command to every Muslim to make a will.

The Sheikh discusses the "Position of Aaron" (Hadith al-Manzila). When the Prophet was leaving for the Battle of Tabuk, he left Ali in charge of Medina. Ali, wanting the honor of Jihad, asked why he was being left behind with the women and children. The Prophet said:
Are you not satisfied to be to me as Aaron was to Moses, except that there is no Prophet after me?

This is a **"Legislative Snapshot."** Aaron was the brother, the partner, and the successor of Moses. By making this comparison, the Prophet gave Ali every rank of Aaron—leadership, authority, and the right to represent the Messenger—and only excluded Prophecy because the message was complete.

The critics say: "This was only for the duration of that battle." But the Sheikh explains the logic of the Message: Does a Prophet use the status of another Prophet [Aaron] as a "temporary job description"? No. When a Prophet defines a rank, he is defining a "Reality of Religion" that is permanent. This was the Prophet’s way of preparing the Ummah to see Ali as the "Aaron of the Muslims." To deny this is to deny the Prophet’s own clarity.
Segment 5: Documentary Theft and Academic Dishonesty

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens his investigation into the "Documentary Theft" performed by major historians. He focuses on the scholar al-Tabari, showing how the specific narrative of Imam Ali’s appointment during the "Day of the Warning" was systematically altered. The Sheikh explains that versions of his history were "cleansed" of the words "Executor" and "Successor" and replaced with "so-and-so." He argues this was a coordinated intellectual effort to prevent the "Reality of Religion" from challenging the "Reality of the World."
We must now look at the **Crime against the Text**. We mentioned the "Day of the Warning" (Yawm al-Inthar). This is one of the most documented events in early Islam, where the Prophet declared:
This is my brother, my executor, and my successor.

However, when we turn to the mainstream books of history, we see a miracle of "editing." Look at the work of **Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari**. In his famous Tarikh (History), in the older manuscripts and his own earlier notes, the words are there. But as the centuries passed, the text changed.
The Sheikh details this distortion. In the widely printed versions of Tabari’s History, the Prophet’s statement is recorded as:
Who among you will support me... to be my brother, and kaza wa kaza (so-and-so and so-and-so)?
They literally replaced the titles Wasi (Executor) and Khalifa (Successor) with vague placeholders.

Think about the psychological effect on a reader. If a seeker of truth reads "so-and-so," they find no evidence. But if they read "my Successor," the entire structure of the Saqifa falls apart. This is why the Sheikh says the File of Ali is a struggle against **Academic Dishonesty**. They didn't just steal the leadership; they tried to steal the memory of the appointment.
The Sheikh notes that even the Great Sahaba (companions) were aware of this censorship. He cites the words of those who stayed loyal to Ali, who would say:
We heard the words of the Prophet with our own ears, yet the scribes of the Sultan write something else.

This is the logic of the World: If you can't kill the man, kill his titles. If you can't deny the event, corrupt the report. By turning Ali’s clear divine rank into an ambiguous "favor," they opened the door for people to claim that the Caliphate was a "political choice" rather than a "heavenly decree." The File of Ali is here to restore the "Executor" to his rightful place.
Segment 6: The Wasi - The ID Card of the Household

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents documents showing a sermon by Imam Hassan (AS) in Sunni sources where he describes his father as the "Successor of the Prophet" (Wasi Rasul Allah). The Sheikh then moves to the battlefield of Ashura, showing that even in the most intense moments of conflict, the companions of Imam Hussain (AS) identified the Household through this title. This segment proves that the concept of the Wasi was a public identity recognized throughout the first century of Islam.

We must ask: did the companions and the following generation actually use these titles, or are they later inventions? The Sheikh provides a document from the books of the "other side." After the martyrdom of the Commander of the Faithful, **Imam Hassan** (peace be upon him) stood on the pulpit and delivered a monumental sermon.
In this sermon, which is narrated in the Musnad of Ahmad and other primary sources, Imam Hassan said:
Yesterday, there departed from among you a man whom those of old did not surpass in knowledge, and whom those of later times will never equal... he was the Successor (Wasi) of the Prophet.

Notice the precision. He didn't just say "my father" or "the fourth Caliph." He used the term that identifies **Ali’s Divine Link to Muhammad**. If this title were a lie, the people in the mosque—many of whom were companions—would have shouted in protest. Their silence was their admission of the "Reality of Religion."

Then we go to the most tragic document: the **Day of Ashura**. We look at the poetry and the war-cries (Rajaz) of the companions of Imam Hussain. When they stepped onto the battlefield to face the army of Yazid, they shouted:
I am the servant of the one who was the Wasi of the Prophet!
They identified themselves as the "Shi'a of the Wasi."

This proves that even fifty years after the Prophet’s death, the title "Wasi" was the banner of the Truth. It was the "ID Card" of the Household. Even the enemies acknowledged it. In their insults, they would say:
O you followers of the Wasi!

They used it as a term of derision, but in doing so, they confirmed the existence of the title. You don't insult someone by a title that doesn't exist. This shows that the **File of Ali** was always open in the hearts of the people. The blood of Ashura was shed to protect the meaning of this one word: **al-Wasi**.
Segment 7: The Private Confessions of the Abbasids

Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores how the title of "Wasi" remained a central point of contention even into the Abbasid era. He cites a letter from Muhammad ibn Abdullah (al-Nafs al-Zakiyya) to the Caliph al-Mansur. Furthermore, the Sheikh reveals a "Private Document" where the Caliph Harun al-Rashid admits to his son (al-Ma'mun) that the Imam of the time (Imam al-Kadhim) is the true owner of the Caliphate, and that the Abbasids are merely usurpers.

The truth of the **Wasiyya** (Succession) did not die with the first generation. It haunted the palaces of the tyrants for centuries. We find the letter of **Muhammad ibn Abdullah al-Mahdh** (known as al-Nafs al-Zakiyya) to the Abbasid Caliph al-Mansur.
In this document, Muhammad ibn Abdullah challenges the Abbasid's right to rule by reminding them:
Our father Ali was the Wasi (Successor) and the Imam... and the inheritance belongs to his children, not to the descendants of the uncle [Abbas].
This shows that the File of Ali was the legal basis for every movement that sought to restore Prophetic justice. Even those who disagreed on political tactics agreed on the identity of the Wasi.

But perhaps the most shocking document comes from **Harun al-Rashid** himself. The Sheikh recounts a narration where Harun was shown great respect by Imam al-Kadhim (peace be upon him). When the Imam left, Harun’s son, al-Ma'mun, asked:
O Commander of the Faithful, who is this man for whom you stood up and showed such honor?
Harun al-Rashid replied:
This is the Imam of the people, the Proof of Allah over His creation, and the Successor of His Messenger.
al-Ma'mun was stunned and asked:
If you know this, then why do you keep him in prison and sit on his throne?
Harun gave the ultimate definition of the **"Reality of the World."** He said:
O my son, power is 'barren' (sterile). By Allah, if you yourself were to struggle with me for this throne, I would take that which holds your eyes—your head—away from you!

This is a confession of the "Criminal Reality." They did not deny Ali’s succession because of a lack of evidence; they denied it because they loved the "Bride" (the World) more than they loved the Truth. They knew exactly who the Wasi was, but they chose to be "Kings" rather than "Believers." The File of Ali records these private confessions as the highest form of evidence against the usurpers.
Segment 8: The Original Sin – Mu'awiyah's Admission

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents the correspondence between Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr and Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan. In this exchange, Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr names Imam Ali (AS) as the "Successor" (Wasi) of the Prophet. The Sheikh highlights how Mu'awiyah’s reply admissions that he and Abu Bakr were the first to "usurp" the right of Ali. The Sheikh then exposes how mainstream historians, once again led by al-Tabari, attempted to censor this letter from the records.

We turn now to a document that is like a lightning bolt in the middle of a dark night. This is the letter of **Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr to Mu'awiyah**. Muhammad was the son of the first Caliph, but he was raised in the lap of Ali, and he knew the "Reality of Religion" better than anyone.
In his letter to Mu'awiyah during the Battle of Siffin, Muhammad wrote:

Our master Ali is the Successor (Wasi) of the Messenger of Allah, the heir to his knowledge, and the first to believe in him... How can you, O son of the 'Liver-Eater' [Hind], compete with the one who was chosen by the Heavens?
Mu'awiyah’s reply is a **Document of Confession**. He wrote back saying:

We do not deny the excellence of the son of Abi Talib... indeed, we saw him with the Prophet like the stars around the moon. But your father [Abu Bakr] and his 'Companion' [Umar] were the first to snatch this matter from him. If they had not done so, we would not have disagreed with him today.

This is the **File of Ali** speaking through the mouths of the enemies. Mu'awiyah is telling the truth of history: the deviation did not start with him in Syria; it started in Medina at the door of Fatima. He is placing the "Original Sin" on the shoulders of those who created the Saqifa.

But look at the "Scholarly Treachery." The Sheikh reveals that when al-Tabari recorded this letter in his history, he did so with a heavy heart. In later editions, this letter was removed or heavily summarized to protect the "Sacred Image" of the early companions. They could not allow the Ummah to read a document where Mu'awiyah admits that the Caliphate was "snatched" from the Wasi.
The Sheikh connects this to the martyrdom of **Hijr ibn Adi**. Hijr was killed by Mu'awiyah because he refused to curse Ali. In the court of Damascus, Hijr stood firm and said:
I will never curse the one whom the Prophet called his Wasi.
This shows that the term "Wasi" was the red line. For the "Reality of the World," this word had to be erased.
Segment 9: The Broken Will and the Cost of Deviation
