Most of the questions were in practical issues that a human is afflicted with in his daily life. They did not ask him about beliefs, about deep knowledge, and they did not ask him about the Proof and about the direction to which the people return. Certainly they did not ask him, because they were in no need of a question, because the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, appointed Ali as a Legatee over this nation after him since the first days of the mission when the verse descended: "And warn your closest kindred." And the Prophet did not leave a situation except that he clarified this truth to them, to the last moment from the moments of his life was the pledge of Al-Ghadir.

And even on the day in which he was martyred, before he was martyred on Thursday, and the Calamity of Thursday is known. The Prophet was martyred on Monday. On Thursday, the issue of the Calamity of Thursday and the issue of the Book, and what Umar said that the Prophet is delirious, and this issue is known. What did the Prophet want to write in this Book? We have in our narrations: **He indeed wrote the book and gave it to the elite of the Shia of Ali, whom he said about that they are the successful ones: Salman, and Abu Dharr, and al-Miqdad.** This is present in our narrations. Just as Ali gathered the Quran and did not give it to the people; when he brought it to the people, they refused to take the Quran from Ali. Just as they refused the Quran of Ali, likewise they refused the book of Muhammad. Ali kept the Quran with him, and the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, wrote the book for the elite of the Shia of Ali. All of this is clarified in our hadiths transmitted from the Ahlulbayt, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all.

So this is the Quran, and this is their explicit statement that the Companions did not ask except these limited issues. And the historical reality, the reality... in tomorrow, the coming episode, God willing the Exalted, I will point to a group of witnesses, and incidents, and events that prove this process with a practical and scientific proof. Revealing to us what the Companions were upon of ignorance and of their lack of knowledge of the rulings and of the limits, which are from the axioms of religious knowledge, let alone the deep knowledge in Monotheism and in the secrets of the Quran! We do not demand of them the deep knowledge of Monotheism and the secrets of the Quran and its sciences; we talk about an issue of legal rulings, an issue of limits, an issue of the rulings of prayer. You find that the Companions do not know Tayammum, they do not know the rulings of doubts in prayer, they do not know the meanings of the Arabic words which the far and the near, the distant and the close know! They do not know their meanings while they recite them. They do not know the rulings of inheritance, they do not know, and they do not know, and they do not know! And the witnesses are not from Shiite books, nor from weak Sunni books. I will bring the witnesses from the considered Sunni books tomorrow in the coming episode. You will see the clear and explicit witnesses that talk about the lack of knowledge of the companions of the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.

As for Ali, he is unique, and Ali remains unique. No one can brand Ali with ignorance. No one can say that Ali was asked and did not know an answer to that question. **Ali is the gate of knowledge and the gate of recognition, and he is the gate of the city.** The gate of the city of knowledge, and the gate of the city of faith, and the gate of the city of wisdom. As for the speech of Al-Sawa'iq al-Muhriqah in that Abu Bakr is its foundation, and that Umar is its walls, and that Uthman is its roof, we will leaf through the books of the people tomorrow to see what is the foundation, and what are the walls, and what is the roof, from the books of the people, not from the books of others, and from the considered books. This is the first trait from the traits of the Imam and from the traits of the Caliph: Knowledge. I will not complete the speech on this topic in this episode; the completion of the speech comes to us continuously, and the episodes of this file support one another. The topics and the researches are intertwined, they come to us successively one after the other, God willing the Exalted.

This is the topic of knowledge. As for the topic of courage, is there any doubt regarding Ali, and that he is the bravest in the time of the Prophet, and before his time, and after his time, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family? Is there any doubt that he is the bravest of the people of his time? Does anyone doubt this truth? A clear issue, the Quran also states that explicitly if we wanted to go to the Quran. The Quran clarifies this truth to us. If we go to Surah al-Ahzab, and we read in Surah al-Ahzab, the tenth verse and what follows it:

When they came at you from above you and from below you, and when eyes shifted and hearts reached the throats, and you assumed about Allah [various] assumptions. There the believers were tested and shaken with a severe earthquake.

No one can say that Ali is in this verse: "When they came at you from above you and from below you, and when eyes shifted and hearts reached the throats, and you assumed about Allah [various] assumptions." Where is your certainty? Where is your faith while you think ill of Allah? "There the believers were tested and shaken with a severe earthquake. And when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease said, Allah and His Messenger did not promise us except delusion. And when a faction of them said, O people of Yathrib, there is no stability for you, so return. And a faction of them asked permission of the Prophet, saying, Indeed, our houses are unprotected, while they were not unprotected. They did not intend except to flee. And if they had been entered upon from all its sides, then they had been asked to commit treason, they would have done it and would not have hesitated over it except briefly. And they had already promised Allah before not to turn their backs and flee. And ever is the promise to Allah that about which one will be questioned. Say, Flight will not benefit you if you should flee from death or killing; and then you would not be given enjoyment except for a little."

These verses talk about the Companions. They talk about fleeing, and about ill thought, and about fear, and about cowardice. What do these verses talk about? They talk about courage! Can anyone say that Ali is within these verses? That Ali is in this verse? "And Allah repelled those who disbelieved in their rage," just as we read a short while ago in their narrations, "they did not obtain any good. **And Allah sufficed the believers in fighting by Ali.**" They wrote thus in their books! Ali is in this verse, this is the verse of Ali. As for these verses: "And hearts reached the throats, and you assumed about Allah [various] assumptions," "and shaken with a severe earthquake," "Indeed, our houses are unprotected, while they were not unprotected, they did not intend except to flee," "And they had already promised Allah before not to turn their backs and flee," but they turned their backs! "Say, Flight will not benefit you if you should flee from death or killing."

Ali is not here. Ali did not pass from here. Everyone passed from here. Ali passed from here: "And Allah repelled those who disbelieved in their rage, they did not obtain any good. And Allah sufficed the believers in fighting"—He sufficed them by Ali. **"And Allah is Strong, Mighty."** A clear issue. Even if we wanted to turn away from these verses and narrations that passed, and the recitation of Ibn Abbas and Ibn Mas'ud, and these sources present in their books. If we read the history of the event, the event was completed and ended, and the victory came by the hand of Haydar, not by the hand of anyone else! O repeated attacker! O repeated attacker! On the day of the repeated attack, your essence knows all things, but it does not know the meaning of fleeing! O repeated attacker! O repeated attacker! On the day of the repeated attack, your essence knows all things, but it does not know the meaning of fleeing! Haydar, Haydar, O conqueror of Khaybar, indeed you do not know the meaning of fleeing, indeed you know the meaning of the repeated attack. Others know the meaning of fleeing. O repeated attacker on the day of the repeated attack! "And Allah sufficed the believers in fighting by Ali," prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.

And the matter is not far when we go... when we go to Surah al-Tawbah, and we go to the 25th verse: **"And on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, but it did not avail you at all, and the earth, as spacious as it is, narrowed for you; then you turned back, fleeing."** And Ali is not here. Ali did not pass from here! "And the earth, as spacious as it is, narrowed for you; then you turned back, fleeing." Tomorrow we will talk about that repeated attacker who is not a fleer (Al-Karrar ghayr al-Farrar). We will talk, God willing, if there is time, about Khaybar, and about that repeated attacker who is not a fleer. Thus Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, described him. So that we may see who is the repeated attacker and who is the fleer. We will talk about Khaybar, we will leaf through history. There are many demands that are not said and not mentioned about the event of Khaybar; we will talk about them, God willing, if there is ample time tomorrow in the fourth episode. And if not, then in the fifth episode.

These verses are clear and explicit. Whoever wants to live in their atmosphere, the courage of Ali becomes manifest to him. "And Allah sufficed the believers in fighting"—He sufficed them by Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. And I here do not want to deal with the historical events, and how the event of Al-Ahzab took place, and how what happened happened in the event of Hunayn on the day "you turned back, fleeing," O companions of the Prophet. The verse explicitly addresses the Companions! And some of the Companions reached in their flight until they arrived at Mecca. And this is mentioned in the books of the people, not in our books. In their books of hadiths, and history, and biographies.

Ali is the most knowledgeable, Ali is the bravest, and Ali is the infallible. Bring me one mistake in the history of Ali! When did Ali make a mistake? **Ali is a puzzle whose beginning is not known and whose end is not known.** Ali, whoever wants to look into his history will acknowledge his infallibility without the need to research in theology or research in the chains of narration of hadiths or the sources of hadiths. A look into the biography of Ali informs you of his infallibility. I do not want to talk here about infallibility; God willing, in another time, perhaps we will have a special file for infallibility. In another time, on another occasion, I will dedicate a special file to the topic of infallibility, because this is also a large topic.

Ali is the most generous. Who is more generous than Ali? "She said: So regarding whom did 'Has there come upon man' (Surah Al-Insan/Al-Dahr) descend in honor? May Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon Al-Sahib ibn Abbad in his poem Al-Manaqib. He said: "She said: So regarding whom did 'Has there come upon man' descend in honor? I said: The most giving of the people of the earth for the supererogatory." He is the most generous, and he is the most knowledgeable.

Come with me, let us leaf through the Quran, just as we leafed through the Quran regarding the knowledge of Ali: "Say, Sufficient is Allah as a witness between me and you, and whoever has knowledge of the Book." Just as we leafed through the Quran regarding the knowledge of the Companions and their questions: "They ask you, they ask you..." this limited number of questions that reveals the extent of their knowledge. Just as we leafed through the verses in Surah al-Ahzab and in Surah Bara'ah about the flight of the Companions and about Ali, and the Quran tells us about his courage. Let us leaf through the Quran so that it tells us about the generosity of Ali. And who is more generous than Ali? **If generosity were a picture embodying itself on the face of this earth, this picture would be Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.**

In Surah al-Baqarah, the 274th verse, verse 274: **"Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and publicly, they will have their reward with their Lord, and there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve."** The verse is present in our narrations and in the narrations of the people that it is regarding Ali, prayers of Allah be upon him. "Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and publicly." He used to spend wealth by night and day, and secretly and publicly. His generosity was in all directions. His generosity did not take a specific form. The generosity of Ali in all times and in all modalities. The verse wants to point to this issue. "They spend their wealth by night and day," in every time, there is not a moment in which Ali is not generous. "By night and day, secretly and publicly," and in whatever modality it was, in the modality of secrecy and in the modality of publicness. "Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and publicly."

In Surah al-Ma'idah, the 55th verse: **"Your guardian is only Allah and His Messenger and those who have believed, those who establish prayer and give Zakat while they are bowing."** Generosity even in the state of prayer and worship! Ali is generosity. If generosity were a picture, this picture would be dust under the feet of Ali. "And those who have believed, those who establish prayer and give Zakat while they are bowing." They spend by night—by night and day, and secretly and publicly. The verse passed a short while ago: "by night and day, secretly and publicly." The verse here talks even while he is in the station of worship, and Ali is in the station of worship, nothing distracts him from Allah, yet while he is bowing, his generosity is connected, emanating, because Ali is generosity, and generosity is Ali.
The verse in Surah... in Surah... in Surah al-Mujadilah. The thirteenth verse and the twelfth verse.

O you who have believed, when you privately consult the Messenger, present before your consultation a charity. That is better for you and purer. But if you find not [the means], then indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful. Have you feared to present before your consultation charities? Then when you do not and Allah has forgiven you, then establish prayer...

to the end of the verse. This verse is known. This is a form from the forms of spending. Allah the Glorified and Exalted obligated the Muslims to pay money when they wanted to visit the Messenger of Allah, to converse with the Messenger of Allah. Where is the generosity of the Companions? All of them held back and did not pay! The only one who paid, pays the charity and comes to visit the Messenger of Allah, and this verse no one participated in with Ali, never.

This is the exegesis of al-Tabari. This is the twenty-seventh and twenty-eighth volume from the exegesis of al-Tabari, the known exegesis. In the tail of this verse: "O you who have believed, when you privately consult the Messenger, present before your consultation a charity." Page 50, page 25 from the twenty-eighth volume. With his chain of narration from Mujahid regarding His saying: "present before your consultation a charity." He said:

They were forbidden from privately consulting the Prophet until they gave charity. They were forbidden from privately consulting the Prophet until they gave charity. So no one privately consulted him except Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him. He presented a dinar and gave it in charity. Then the concession was revealed regarding that.
Another hadith from Mujahid, with his chain of narration from Mujahid. He said: "Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, said:

Indeed, in the Book of Allah the Mighty and Majestic there is a verse which no one acted upon before me, and no one will act upon after me: O you who have believed, when you privately consult the Messenger, present before your consultation a charity. He said: It was obligated, then it was abrogated.

**No one acted upon it from the Companions. Where is their generosity? Where is the generosity of the Companions?** The hadith books that they say about them, so-and-so spent such and such, and so-and-so freed so-and-so... these are things we do not acknowledge. We treat them with the treatment of chains of narration and sources as they want. As for this, it is Quran. Quran! And this is an established and known issue. This is a verse that talks about generosity, and about the lack of generosity of the Companions.

"O you who have believed, when you privately consult the Messenger, present before your consultation a charity." He said: "They were forbidden from privately consulting the Prophet until they gave charity. So no one privately consulted him except Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him." From Qatadah, he said: "The people asked the Messenger of Allah until they overwhelmed him, so Allah admonished them with this verse. And a man would have a need for the Prophet of Allah, so he could not fulfill it until he presented a charity before him. So that became severe upon them. Then Allah the Mighty and Majestic revealed the concession after that."

The verses are explicit. "Have you feared to present before your consultation charities?" Meaning have you felt pity for yourselves? Have you feared for your wealth, and did not feel pity for the Messenger of Allah? This is the meaning of the verse. "Have you feared to present before your consultation charities?" A visit to the Messenger of Allah, they could not pay a dirham? Is this the value of the Messenger of Allah to them? I do not know. The verse is clear and explicit. This is a situation. I do not say that the value of the Messenger of Allah to all the Companions in all times is like this, but this is a test. This is a test from the tests. It does not mean that the Companions did not have situations in which they had clear generosity; I do not deny these truths. There are truths present that we cannot deny of the generosity of the Companions in other situations. **But who is the most generous? The question is about the most generous, about the bravest.** I do not say that the Companions, all the Companions, were not brave. And I do not say that the Companions, all the Companions, did not have knowledge, never, I do not say this. But I search and talk about the most knowledgeable. Otherwise, we have in the narrations that Salman was given the first and the last knowledge. And there are among the Companions those who possess vast knowledge. But the talk is about the most knowledgeable. The talk is about the bravest. The talk is about the most generous. I do not say that the Companions had no generosity, but this verse reveals who the most generous is. The most generous is Ali.

The verses talked: he spends by night and day, secretly and publicly. He gives charity while he is bowing. When the command to give charity before private consultation descended, he is the one who gave charity, and no one else gave charity. All of this reveals the generosity. And the generosity of Ali does not stop at this limit. When we go to Surah al-Dahr or Surah al-Insan: **"And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive. We only feed you for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude."** This is generosity! We do not want reward or gratitude. We do not want a reward in exchange for this generosity, and we do not want thanks or praise. Why? Because generosity is an intrinsic quality in them, in the Ahlulbayt. An intrinsic quality in Ali and the family of Ali. And the intrinsic quality manifests without a cause, like perfume. Like a bottle of perfume: when the bottle of perfume is opened, the place fills with the good scent. Why? Because the nature of the perfume is that it spreads. The perfume here does not need praise; whether you praise the perfume or do not praise the perfume, the perfume spreads. The light of the sun, when it rises, it does not need you to say that this light is illuminating; it points to itself by itself.

"And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive. We only feed you for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude." And therefore, what is the reward for this generosity? **"Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup whose mixture is of Kafur." "And they will be given to drink a cup whose mixture is of Zanjabil."** Until the verse says: **"And their Lord will give them a purifying drink."** This purifying drink is for whom? This purifying drink is for the purified essences. When we go to Surah al-Waqi'ah: "Indeed, it is a noble Quran. In a Register well-protected. None touch it except the purified." These are the ones who drink the purifying drink. And this noble Quran is their drink, it is this: "Indeed, it is a noble Quran. In a Register well-protected. None touch it except the purified." "And their Lord will give them a purifying drink." These purified ones, the purified ones drink the purifying drink. **Who gives them to drink? Their Lord.** Not the angels. "And their Lord will give them to drink." The cupbearer here is their Lord, is Allah. **"And their Lord will give them a purifying drink."** This is the drink of Ali and the family of Ali.

As for the drink of his enemies, in the same Surah al-Waqi'ah: "Then indeed you, O those astray [who are] deniers"—an address to the enemies of Ali, thus our Imams said, it is not I who says it—"will be eating from trees of Zaqqum, and filling with it your bellies, and drinking on top of it from scalding water, and will drink as the drinking of thirsty camels (Al-Hīm)." Al-Hīm: the thirsty, frantic camels. When the camels become frantic, they see the water, and they are thirsty, frantic, and agitated; they trample one another. "Then indeed you, O those astray [who are] deniers, will be eating from trees of Zaqqum, and filling with it your bellies, and drinking on top of it from scalding water, and will drink as the drinking of thirsty camels. That is their accommodation on the Day of Recompense." "And their Lord will give them a purifying drink." The exact same Surah. "Indeed, it is a noble Quran" in Surah al-Waqi'ah, "In a Register well-protected, none touch it except the purified." And the verses of the Book support one another, and the hadith of the Ahlulbayt with the verses of the Book support one another. The verses of the Book with the words of the Ahlulbayt produce for us the solid structure. Which structure is this? The structure of the Book and the Progeny. The Two Weighty Things: "As long as you hold fast to them." There is a solid structure, we build this solid structure from the Book and the Progeny, from the Two Weighty Things: "As long as you hold fast to them, you will never go astray after me."

This is the generosity of Ali, "we do not want from you reward or gratitude." You notice different referents, different pictures of the generosity of Ali, until the generosity of Ali reaches its most beautiful picture in the 207th verse from Surah al-Baqarah: **"And of the people is he who sells himself, seeking means to the approval of Allah."** Sells... sells! This verse descended regarding Ali when he spent the night in the bed of the Messenger of Allah, the night of the stay. "She said: So who is the one who spent the night upon the bed as a ransom?" And this is the courage of Ali also; this is his generosity and his courage here. "She said: So who is the one who spent the night upon the bed as a ransom? So I said: The most steadfast of Allah's creation in fear." "And of the people is he who sells himself, seeking means to the approval of Allah. And Allah is kind to [His] servants." This is the generosity of Ali, this is another picture from the pictures of the generosity of Ali. Until the clearest meanings of the generosity of Ali manifest in Surah Al Imran, in the 61st verse: **"Then whoever argues with you about it after what has come to you of knowledge, then say: Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, our selves and your selves."** This is his generosity. The generosity with the soul is the utmost limit of generosity! Until he became a soul for the Messenger of Allah. This is generosity. This is generosity in a deep meaning. This is a generosity that we do not perceive. This is not generosity with wealth, nor generosity with the soul in the arenas of fighting. This is generosity of another meaning. This is the highest meaning of generosity! This is the generosity of Ali for Muhammad, and the generosity of Muhammad for Ali. Muhammad said: "I am Ali, and Ali is me," and Ali said: "I am Muhammad, and Muhammad is me." Peace be upon you, O you who are Muhammad, and Muhammad is you, O Master of the Legatees.

These are breezes from the fragrance of the Noble Book. And something of truths and documents. Clear, evident truths and documents from the books of those who were harsh to Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, and turned away from him. Clear truths, as is written in the introduction of the program: irrefutable truths, and a screaming oppression. And I do not want to talk here about the oppression of Ali. **The oppression of Ali is greater than me, and greater than any speech.** The oppression of Ali, the Messenger of Allah knows it. The oppression of Ali, Ali knows it. Al-Zahra knows it. And therefore, what al-Zahra offered was a sacrifice in the arena of the oppression of Ali. Al-Zahra, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her, was a sacrifice in the arena of the oppression of Ali. She is the witness, the martyr. And she is the first who defended the courtyard of Ali, the sacred courtyard of the Imamate.

The completion of the talk comes to us successively, God willing, in the coming episodes. Tomorrow, the same time, the same place, the same screen, and the same loyalty. Our appointment is renewed with you with Ali and the family of Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all. I will meet you upon good. In the safety of Allah.
